data.title: Cboe multiply-listed options ADV slides 4% M/M, gains 28% Y/Y in May
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) multiply-listed options average daily volume ("ADV") of 12.7M contracts slid 4.1% from a month earlier, but climbed 27.6% from the year-ago period, the derivatives exchange said on Wednesday.

Index options ADV totaled 4.33M contracts last month, down 14.9% from April and up 14.7% from May 2024.

Futures contracts ADV dropped 46.4% sequentially and 22.5% Y/Y to 166K contracts.

U.S. equities - on exchange - ADV was 1.86B matched shares, down from 2.12B in April and up from 1.42B a year before.

Canadian equities ADV of 135.1M matched shares compared with 170.5M in April and 140.2M in last year's May.

European equities ADV stood at €12.1B vs. €17.4B in April and €8.63B in May 2024.

data.title: Canadian Natural Resources restarts oil sands operation as wildfire danger eases
data.description:
Canadian Natural Resources (NYSE:CNQ) said late Tuesday it restarted its Jackfish 1 oil sands site in northern Alberta after a second day of rain slowed the spread of wildfires.

Canada's biggest oil producer said its operation at the site will ramp up over the next several days, targeting full production of ~36,500 bbl/day by Friday, after it had evacuated non-essential workers and halted production as a precaution last weekend due to wildfires burning south of Fort McMurray.

Conditions have since improved from conditions last week that forced evacuations and shut as much as 7% of Canada's daily oil production.

Still, Bloomberg reported that ~462K bbl/day of oil production is within 10 km of a total of 25 wildfires that burn out of control in oil-producing Alberta province.

As of Wednesday morning, 238K bbl/day of production remained halted at Christina Lake oil sands facilities operated by Cenovus Energy (CVE) and MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), although there have been no reports of significant damage to oil infrastructure or company assets due to the fires.

data.title: Cullinan Therapeutics in pact with Genrix Bio for velinotamig
data.description:
Cullinan Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CGEM) signed a deal with Genrix Bio for an exclusive license for velinotamig, a bispecific T cell engager. 

As per the deal, Cullinan will pay Genrix Bio a $20M upfront license fee for exclusive rights to develop and commercialize velinotamig in all disease areas worldwide other than Greater China. 

Genrix can earn up to $292M in development and regulatory milestones and an extra $400M based on sales, alongside tiered royalties ranging from the mid-single digits to the mid-teens on potential net sales outside Greater China.

Genrix aims to start an early stage trial in China by year-end for patients with autoimmune conditions.Cullinan plans to use this data to speed up the program's clinical development.

Cullinan also confirmed it has enough cash to operate until 2028.

data.title: Viewbix to uplist to Nasdaq Capital Market on June 5
data.description:
• Viewbix (OTCPK:VBIX) announced that its common stock has been approved for listing on Nasdaq.
  • Shares will begin trading under the symbol “VBIX” on or about June 5, 2025.
  • Previously, the stock was quoted on the OTC Pink Tier, where trading ceased on June 4, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Minco Capital announces grant of stock options
data.description:
• Minco Capital (TSXV:MMM:CA) has approved the grant of an aggregate of 2.7M incentive stock options to directors, officers, and consultants of the company, pursuant to its stock option plan.
  • The options will expire five years from the date of grant and are subject to vesting over a period of 18 months in accordance with the company's stock option plan.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NeuroPace secures $75M credit facility with MidCap Financial
data.description:
• NeuroPace (NASDAQ:NPCE) entered into a new $75M credit facility with MidCap Financial, comprising a $60M term loan and $15M revolving credit facility.
  • Proceeds from the term loan were used to fully repay an existing loan with CRG Partners IV, L.P.
  • The revolving credit facility will support working capital and corporate initiatives.
  • The financing provides non-dilutive capital at improved terms, reducing cash interest expense.
  • The new facility has 5-year maturity.
  • NPCE shares rise over 4% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge CFO to step down
data.description:
• Lulu’s Fashion Lounge (NASDAQ:LVLU) announced that Tiffany R. Smith, chief financial officer, is stepping down from her role effective June 30, 2025.
  • Upon Smith’s departure, Crystal Landsem, chief executive officer, will assume the responsibilities of interim chief financial officer, alongside her responsibilities as chief executive officer. 
  • Source: Press release
